_note: Per-lesson role-play scenario. Grounded in A1/012 dialogue + vocab. Learner plays Emma; computer plays Ben. Ben tells about the beach, Emma asks the questions. Each NPC node offers 2-3 branches so the same lesson language can be reached different ways. Consumed by RP.Engine + RP.classifiers.local (see roleplay/).
title: 012 · The Sandcastle

npc.text: We went to the beach on Sunday.

routes.meaning: Emma asks if Ben swam in the sea.
examples: Did you swim in the sea?
Did you go in the water?
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Did you swim in the sea?”

routes.meaning: Emma says she loves the beach.
routes.hint: 💬 You can ask “Did you swim in the sea?”

partial.message: Ask about the beach: “Did you swim in the sea?”

off_topic.message: Ben went to the beach. Ask “Did you swim in the sea?”

npc.text: Me too! The water was so nice. Ask me if we went swimming!

routes.meaning: Emma asks if Ben swam in the sea.
examples: Did you swim in the sea?
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Did you swim in the sea?”

partial.message: Ask if he swam: “Did you swim in the sea?”

off_topic.message: Ask Ben “Did you swim in the sea?”

npc.text: Yes, and we built a big sandcastle.

routes.meaning: Emma asks how big the sandcastle was.
Emma asks who helped build it.
routes.hint: 💬 You can ask “How big was it?”

partial.message: Ask about the castle: “How big was it?”

off_topic.message: Ben built a sandcastle. Ask “How big was it?”

npc.text: My brother helped me. It got really tall, too!

routes.meaning: Emma asks how big the sandcastle was.

partial.message: Ask how big: “How big was it?”

npc.text: It was taller than my little brother!

routes.meaning: Emma asks if they found shells.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Did you find any shells?”

partial.message: Ask about shells: “Did you find any shells?”

off_topic.message: The castle was tall. Ask “Did you find any shells?”

routes.meaning: Emma asks if the waves broke the castle.
examples: Did the waves break your castle?
Did the sea break your castle?
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Did the waves break your castle?”

routes.meaning: Emma asks how many shells they found.
routes.hint: 💬 You can ask “Did the waves break your castle?”

partial.message: Ask about the waves: “Did the waves break your castle?”

off_topic.message: They found many shells. Ask “Did the waves break your castle?”

npc.text: So many, my hands were full! Then the sea came close.

learning_objective: Ask if the waves broke the castle

routes.meaning: Emma asks if the waves broke the castle.
examples: Did the waves break your castle?
So did the waves break it?
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Did the waves break your castle?”

partial.message: Ask about the waves: “Did the waves break your castle?”

off_topic.message: The sea came close. Ask “Did the waves break your castle?”

npc.text: Yes, but we laughed and built another one.

routes.meaning: Emma says the beach is the best place in summer.
examples: The beach is the best place in summer!
Summer at the beach is the best!
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “The beach is the best place in summer!”

routes.meaning: Emma asks to go to the beach together.
examples: Let's go to the beach together!
routes.hint: 💬 You can say “The beach is the best place in summer!”

partial.message: Give a comment: “The beach is the best place in summer!”

off_topic.message: They built another castle. Say “The beach is the best place in summer!”

npc.text: It really is the best! Let's go again soon. Bye, Emma!
Yes! Let's build a sandcastle together next time. See you, Emma!
